pageTitle.value: Michael Ondaatje and Miroslav Penkov - Video

pageCaption.value: Bulgarian protégé Miroslav Penkov could not believe that his mentor was Michael Ondaatje, “someone who stands so tall in a field of giants”. For Ondaatje, author of The English Patient, “our lives are utterly disordered”, so watching Penkov bring order into life by writing fiction was a source of pleasure.

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégé in Literature, 2014–2015

text.value: Bulgarian protégé Miroslav Penkov could not believe that his mentor was Michael Ondaatje, “someone who stands so tall in a field of giants”. For Ondaatje, author of The English Patient, “our lives are utterly disordered”, so watching Penkov bring order into life by writing fiction was a source of pleasure.

pageTitle.value: Wole Soyinka and Tara June Winch - Video

pageCaption.value: Declaring their mentorship to be “a process of development”, Wole Soyinka, first African Laureate of the Nobel Prize for Literature, encourages his protégée, Tara June Winch, as she agonizes over her writing. When anyone writes, he says, himself included, “it’s all over the place.” It’s the rewriting that follows that marks out a true writer.

WaPageTitle.value: Wole Soyinka and Tara June Winch - Video

pageTitle.value: Mia Couto and Julián Fuks - Video

pageCaption.value: Mia Couto’s mentoring relationship with Julián Fuks was a fusion of interests wrought by their mutual experiences as writers living in former colonies of Portugal. Liberated from his “autofiction” style of writing by his mentor, Fuks found he was able to explore his imagination in a different way.

WaPageTitle.value: Mia Couto and Julián Fuks - Video

pageTitle.value: Hans Magnus Enzensberger and Tracy K. Smith - Video

pageCaption.value: In a mentorship that they describe as an intense and joyful conversation, poets Hans Magnus Enzensberger (from Germany) and Tracy K. Smith (American) explore the nature of poetry – “Everyone has poetry in their head,” Enzensberger proclaims. But their quest goes further, examining history and identity as Smith realizes a longstanding desire to write her family memoir.

WaPageTitle.value: Hans Magnus Enzensberger and Tracy K. Smith - Video

pageTitle.value: Kaija Saariaho and Vasco Mendonça - Video

pageCaption.value: Composing music is a solitary occupation. For Finland’s Kaija Saariaho and her protégé, Portugal’s Vasco Mendonça the mentorship delivered a rare opportunity to “enrich” each other discussing music in concert halls around Europe and in the United States.

WaPageTitle.value: Kaija Saariaho and Vasco Mendonça - Video

pageTitle.value: Jennifer Tipton and Sebastián Solórzano Rodríguez - Video

pageCaption.value: Jennifer Tipton, one of the world’s greatest lighting designers, declares that “light is the substance of our existence”. Her protégé, Mexico’s Sebastián Solórzano Rodríguez, agrees completely as he witnessed Tipton creating lighting for performances at theatres and opera houses all over the world.

pageTitle.value: Olafur Eliasson and Sammy Baloji - Video

pageCaption.value: For Sammy Baloji, who had mainly worked in photography, immersion in the life and work of mentor and multimedia artist Olafur Eliasson brought a transformation, enriching his work and bringing a greater complexity of language as he discovered new materials and modes of expression.
